title: RapidML Study Config
description: The runtime configuration object produced by normalizeConfig() in core/generator.js and consumed by every plugin (methodologies, templates, adapters). This is NOT a saved file format — it is the internal JavaScript object that flows through the wizard after the user fills the form and clicks Generate Package.

db.description: Database dialect. Controls SQL syntax differences (date literals, INTERVAL vs DATEADD, generate_series vs ROW_NUMBER, temp table style).

schema.description: OMOP CDM schema name used to qualify all table references (e.g. schema.condition_occurrence). Sanitized to [a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.]{0,127} at runtime.

dataModel.description: Registered adapter ID. Selects which data model adapter translates evidence blocks into SQL. Currently only 'omop' is implemented.

startYear.description: Study calendar start year (string, used as-is in date literals). Must be a 4-digit year earlier than endYear.

endYear.description: Study calendar end year (string, used in date literals). Must be later than startYear.

baselineDays.description: Days to look back before the index date for baseline feature computation. Must parse to a positive integer.

outcomeDays.description: Days to look forward after the index date for outcome observation. Must parse to a positive integer.

debug.description: When true, generate step-by-step DROP/CREATE TEMP TABLE statements with row-count checkpoints instead of a single CTE chain. Useful for diagnosing small-cohort or empty-result issues.

bestPracticeMode.description: When true, include a detailed manifest.json in the output zip. Generated by omop/artifacts.js.

methodology.description: ID of the registered RapidML.Methodologies plugin that builds the spine CTE and the full SQL. Built-in: 'longitudinal-prediction' (one row per person per year) and 'single-window' (one row per person).

analysisTemplate.description: ID of the registered RapidML.AnalysisTemplates plugin that generates the Python analysis script. Built-in: 'logistic-regression' (run.py) and 'decision-tree' (run_decision_tree.py).

covariateEncoding.description: Encoding mode for predefined numeric covariates. 'count' = raw count in baseline window; 'binary' = 1/0 flag; 'count_and_binary' = both columns.

covariates.description: Selected predefined covariate IDs to include in the final SELECT. Each ID corresponds to a SQL subquery block in omop/covariates.js.

customCovariates.description: User-defined concept-level covariate features added via the Custom Covariates section. Each entry generates one SQL column in the final SELECT via omop/covariates.js.

studyDefinition.description: The evidence block study definition collected by EvidenceUI.collectStudyDefinition(). Contains four blocks: entry, outcome, exclusions, confounders.

entry.description: Cohort entry block — defines who enters the cohort and sets t0.

match.description: 'all': every row must match (HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT row_idx) = N); uses MAX(event_date) as t0. 'any': at least one row matches (UNION ALL); uses MIN(event_date) as t0.

rows.description: One or more evidence rows. At least one is required — validateConfig() rejects empty entry blocks.

outcome.description: Outcome block — defines the event to predict. At least one row required.

exclusions.description: Patients to remove from the cohort. Each row generates an AND NOT EXISTS(...) clause in the censored spine CTE.

confounders.description: Binary flag features. Each row generates a CASE WHEN EXISTS(...) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END column in the final SELECT.

evidenceRow.description: A single evidence criterion. Processed by the registered data model adapter (omop/evidence-sql.js) into a SQL CTE or subquery.

type.description: Row type. Determines the OMOP table, concept column, and date column queried. See rule-types.json for the full mapping table.

conceptId.description: OMOP concept ID. Non-numeric characters are stripped at runtime via String(val).replace(/[^0-9]/g, ''). Use the concept reference sidebar (right panel) to look up IDs.

descendants.description: When true (and type is not 'lab' or 'visit'), resolve descendant concept IDs via concept_ancestor. Generates an extra _concepts CTE.

operator.description: Comparison operator for lab/observation value threshold. Validated against a whitelist; defaults to '>' if invalid.

value.description: Numeric threshold string for lab/observation rows (e.g. '60' for eGFR < 60). Parsed via parseFloat at runtime.

label.description: Human-readable label used in the generated README and manifest.json.

minCount.description: Minimum number of matching event records required (generates HAVING COUNT(*) >= N). Values <= 1 produce no HAVING clause.

distinctVisits.description: When true, enforce HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT visit_occurrence_id) >= minCount instead of raw row count.

visitContext.description: Filter events by visit type. 'all' = no filter. 'inpatient' = concept 9201, 'outpatient' = 9202, 'emergency' = 9203, 'custom' = visitContextIds array.

visitContextIds.description: Custom visit_concept_id values when visitContext is 'custom'.

customCovariate.description: A user-defined OMOP concept feature added via the wizard Custom Covariates section. Processed by omop/covariates.js after the predefined covariate loop.

domain.description: OMOP domain — maps to the corresponding table (condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, measurement, procedure_occurrence, observation, visit_occurrence).

conceptId.description: OMOP concept ID (digits only — non-numeric characters are stripped at runtime).

aggregation.description: How to compute the feature within the baseline window. 'count' = COUNT(*); 'binary' = 1 if any match else 0; 'last_value'/'first_value'/'min_value'/'max_value' = value_as_number aggregations.

label.description: SQL column alias. Sanitised to lowercase [a-z0-9_] at runtime, truncated to 50 chars. Auto-generated as domain_conceptId if empty.
